core_mechanism: a cooperative phase transition among coupled two-state units on a heterogeneous network whose critical threshold and transition order are governed by the moments of the connectivity (degree) distribution, so that divergence of a moment (fat/heavy tails) shifts the critical point and changes the universality/order of the transition
brief_reason: The abstract computes an exact critical point on a random graph with arbitrary degree distribution P(k) and shows that whether the fourth or second moment of P(k) diverges controls anomalous critical behavior, the location of the threshold, and even makes the transition of infinite order.
